description: Subsample=1.0 control: deterministic XGBoost training eliminates stochasticity, removing the Rashomon set, which should eliminate flip rate instability. Compared against subsample=0.8 (stochastic).
verdict: Deterministic flip rate = 0.0000 (CI: [0.0000, 0.0010]), stochastic flip rate = 0.2282 (CI: [0.2151, 0.2418]). Mechanism confirmed: stochastic training creates Rashomon set.
